refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall ensure teleoperation communication latency is maintained at or below 150 ms p95 to enable safe remote operation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR specifies a high-level latency requirement for teleoperation. Refining it to explicitly state the latency threshold and its impact on safety clarifies the performance target and safety criticality.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure end-to-end communication latency under agreed network conditions using telemetry data during teleoperation sessions and verify p95 latency ≤ 150 ms.
sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication latency in the teleoperation data path.
FR2: Monitor and report latency metrics in real-time.
FR3: Manage network link quality to maintain latency targets.

candidate_dps: DP1: Optimize data encoding and compression algorithms to reduce payload size.
DP2: Implement priority-based network traffic scheduling for teleoperation data.
DP3: Deploy latency monitoring and adaptive control mechanisms.
DP4: Use dedicated network link with QoS guarantees.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP2: Implement priority-based network traffic scheduling for teleoperation data.

potential_sources: Interaction between network scheduling and safety services.
coupling_check.notes: DP2 logically isolates teleoperation traffic prioritization, minimizing coupling with other system functions while supporting latency requirements.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network infrastructure supports traffic prioritization.
Risk of degraded performance if network conditions worsen beyond assumptions.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating adaptive control (DP3) if static scheduling proves insufficient under variable network conditions.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for teleoperation data handling, including traffic scheduling and latency management.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Interface for sending and receiving teleoperation data packets with prioritized scheduling.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Interface to report latency metrics and communication status to monitoring systems.

module_containment_scores.notes: Broad scope but less focused on teleoperation-specific latency control.
Physical vehicle module has minimal direct control over teleoperation communication scheduling.
Highest containment score due to direct responsibility for teleoperation data handling and latency management.
Supports safety alerts but not directly responsible for latency control.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Assigning DP2 to the Teleoperation module centralizes latency control, reducing diffusion and improving maintainability.
containment_risks: Potential interface complexity with Network Link if network infrastructure changes.
Dependency on accurate telemetry data for latency monitoring.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Review interface definitions with Network Link team to ensure compatibility and support for traffic prioritization.

fr_dp_gate.reason: Selected DP (priority-based network traffic scheduling) directly addresses the refined FR of maintaining latency ≤ 150 ms with clear verification paths.

diffusion_risks: Minimal diffusion risk as DP is contained within Teleoperation module.
Interface dependencies with Network Link and Telemetry are well-defined.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores support Teleoperation as the best ownership candidate, ensuring clear module boundaries.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Latency can be verified via telemetry interface and network link monitoring within the Teleoperation module.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of latency control DP to Teleoperation IM with well-defined external interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Clarify interface protocols with Network Link to support traffic prioritization.
Consider fallback mechanisms if network QoS is degraded.

decision.justification: The current level of decomposition sufficiently refines the FR, selects a viable DP, and assigns it to a suitable IM with clear interfaces and manageable risks. Verification and containment are feasible.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication latency in the teleoperation data path.
FR2: Monitor and report latency metrics in real-time.
FR3: Manage network link quality to maintain latency targets.

revise.why: No immediate revision needed; proceed to next decomposition level.

assumptions: Telemetry data is accurate and timely.
open_risks: Network conditions may degrade beyond assumptions affecting latency.
Interface changes in Network Link could impact implementation.
evidence_needed: Latency measurements under operational network conditions.
